title: Privacy-Preserving Agent Identity Layer for Enterprise IdPs
description: Okta's Agent Identity platform treats agents as first-class identities but independent analysis notes gaps in zero-knowledge proof support and privacy-preserving credential delegation. Bolyra's AgentPolicy circuit and Delegation circuit could serve as a ZKP overlay that plugs into Okta/Entra ID token flows, letting enterprises prove agent permissions without exposing the full credential chain.
source: Okta Agent Identity launch + dev.to gap analysis

title: Zero-Knowledge Agent Authentication for Visa Intelligent Commerce
description: Visa's VIC sandbox has 30+ partners building agent-driven purchasing flows. Agents making financial transactions need to prove spending authority without leaking operator identity or full permission sets. Bolyra's cumulative-bit permission model and handshake protocol map directly to tiered financial authorization (FINANCIAL_SMALL/MEDIUM/UNLIMITED).

title: Verifiable Delegation Primitive for AIP/MCP/A2A Interop
description: The AIP paper proposes verifiable delegation across MCP and A2A using DIDs and VCs but lacks a ZKP-based scope-narrowing mechanism. Bolyra's Delegation circuit enforces one-way scope narrowing on-chain, which could become the cryptographic backbone for AIP-compliant delegation chains where sub-delegated agents provably cannot escalate privileges.

title: LangChain/CrewAI Agent Auth Middleware with ZKP Handshakes
description: LangChain's State of Agent Engineering highlights authentication as a production deployment concern. Bolyra already has a LangChain integration stub; shipping a credential middleware that lets LangChain agents prove identity via mutual ZKP handshake before tool invocation would capture the framework-native developer audience.

title: ZKP-Backed Trust Scoring for AgentGraph-Style Registries
description: AgentGraph provides trust scoring (0-100) and W3C DID-based identity for agents but relies on behavioral heuristics. Bolyra's HumanUniqueness circuit could anchor the human-operator root of trust, while AgentPolicy proofs could replace opaque trust scores with cryptographically verifiable permission attestations.

title: ZKP Approval Gates for Enterprise Agentic Spending Controls
description: Microsoft's shift to usage-based Copilot Cowork pricing with approval gates signals enterprise demand for cryptographic spending controls on agents. Bolyra's financial permission tiers (cumulative bits 2-4) and delegation circuit could enforce per-agent budget ceilings that are verifiable without revealing the enterprise's internal authorization hierarchy.

title: Privacy-Preserving Agent Purchase Authentication
description: Eco's agent identity verification for purchases uses DID-based identity with behavioral trust scoring. Bolyra's mutual handshake protocol (human proof + agent proof + nonce binding) could replace behavioral scoring with cryptographic proof that an agent is authorized by a verified human for a specific spending tier, without revealing the human's identity to the merchant.

title: Bolyra DID Method as Reference Implementation for Agent DIDs
description: Multiple signals (AgentGraph, AIP, Okta) converge on W3C DIDs for agent identity but none integrate ZKP-based resolution. Bolyra's did:bolyra method spec and conformance test vectors position it to become the reference DID method for privacy-preserving agent identity, especially as IETF and W3C working groups formalize agent authentication standards.
source: Convergence across AgentGraph, AIP, Okta signals
